postings: OpenAI’s 2025 hiring pipeline includes several technical positions such as Machine Learning Engineer, Research Scientist, LLM Engineer, and AI Engineer, all centered on developing, scaling, and improving large‑language‑model systems, alignment research, and deployment infrastructure; candidates are typically expected to hold advanced degrees (Ph.D. or master’s) in computer science, machine learning, or a related field or demonstrate equivalent industry experience, possess deep expertise in Python, PyTorch or TensorFlow, distributed training, and model optimization, and show a track record of publishing or shipping high‑impact AI research or products, with roles offering opportunities to work on next‑generation LLM architectures, safety and policy frameworks, and API‑driven AI services.
velocity: In the most recent 30‑day window of 2025, OpenAI listed a broad slate of AI‑focused openings, featuring roles such as a Research Engineer / Machine Learning Engineer on the Applied Voice team, a Machine Learning Engineer on the Integrity team, AI Deployment Engineers for the ChatGPT Ecosystem and for Start‑up Technical Success, an Android Engineer for ChatGPT Engineering, multiple Software Engineer positions across Applied AI Engineering (including Codex App, Codex Cloud, Compute‑Storage, Cooperative AI Support Automation, Developer Productivity, Full‑Stack Developer Experience, Privacy & Security), and a Solutions Engineer for Retail Technical Success, all of which reflect OpenAI’s ongoing recruitment of talent to advance its machine‑learning and generative‑AI initiatives.
